messages.content:
Count the records satisfying mass >= 5.
Records:
  - jade: mass 3, glow 3, span 3, charge 8
  - ochre: mass 9, glow 2, span 1, charge 2
  - teal: mass 8, glow 5, span 7, charge 1
  - umber: mass 2, glow 9, span 2, charge 9
  - cobalt: mass 6, glow 2, span 3, charge 6
  - indigo: mass 7, glow 1, span 6, charge 6
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer>

Choose the lowest-cost tool that supports every required capability.
Tools:
  - kav: supports exact,stream; cost 7
  - fal: supports exact,stream; cost 3
  - nix: supports exact,stream,table; cost 2
  - jor: supports exact,table,text; cost 3
  - gim: supports exact,image,table,text; cost 1
Required: exact,stream.
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer>

Return an id only when exactly one record satisfies all requirements. If none or more than one qualifies, abstain with INSUFFICIENT.

  - 46: mass 6, glow 9, span 3, charge 9
  - 24: mass 4, glow 5, span 9, charge 7
  - 12: mass 7, glow 6, span 5, charge 5

  - 34: mass 8, glow 4, span 1, charge 8
Requirements: span >= 7 and mass >= 6 and glow <= 4.
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer>

Recover the unique complete order implied by these precedence facts.

  - III occurs before IV
  - IV occurs before I
  - XI occurs before X
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer> (join ids with '>')

Apply this procedure in order to I / VI / X / IV / I / IX:

A worker starts with R · J · J · H · W · R · R and applies: reverse the entire sequence; then overwrite position 3 with M. They claim the result is R · R · M · H · J · J · R. Verify the claim.
Cycle order: H T J C D W R F K M (after the last item, wrap to the first).
End with exactly one line: ANSWER: YES or ANSWER: NO

A worker starts with D ~ K ~ D ~ C ~ T ~ A and applies: advance every item 2 step(s) in the cycle; then reverse the entire sequence; then overwrite position 2 with R; then if the first item is J or C, rotate left once; otherwise rotate right once. They claim the result is T ~ F ~ R ~ C ~ T ~ A. Verify the claim.
Cycle order: H P R J K D A T F C (after the last item, wrap to the first).
End with exactly one line: ANSWER: YES or ANSWER: NO

A worker starts with 57 ~ 79 ~ 12 ~ 02 ~ 46 ~ 12 and applies: advance every item 5 step(s) in the cycle; then reverse the entire sequence; then overwrite position 1 with 02; then swap positions 4 and 6. They claim the result is 02 ~ 79 ~ 68 ~ 46 ~ 12 ~ 02. Verify the claim.
Cycle order: 46 12 68 24 57 79 02 35 80 (after the last item, wrap to the first).

Maintain three registers. Initial state: P=2, W=5, H=8. Apply each instruction to the current state in order.

  2. swap the values of P and W
  3. add 2 to W
  4. swap the values of H and P
  5. move up to 2 from P to W
  6. if P is at least 4, add 2 to H

ANSWER: <answer> (format name=value entries in the original name order, separated by ';')
